source: public web (Google Trends, Reddit, TikTok, news)

trends.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 dominates global attention
trends.summary: The 2026 FIFA World Cup, hosted across the US, Mexico and Canada, is in its most-watched knockout/final stretch, driving mass watch-parties, while the death of South African midfielder Jayden Adams weeks after playing in the tournament has also drawn heavy coverage.
trends.angle: World Cup watch parties mean spilled drinks, snack disasters, muddy jerseys/cleats and stadium-scale mess — a built-in, non-cleaning-forced tie to game-day cleanup.
trends.response: Have Dawn or Swiffer post a same-day reactive 'final whistle, first dish duty' graphic tied to match results, and seed a creator doing a rapid halftime spill-cleanup speed-run set to trending World Cup audio.

examples.appeal: UGC watch-party chaos (spilled beer, jumping on couches) is inherently relatable and remixable for a 'cleanup after the goal' cutaway.
Reels of fan celebrations and jersey-clad crowds offer high-energy backdrops for a quick branded reaction post.
Raw, unscripted fan reaction clips get huge organic reach and are easy to duet/stitch with a branded punchline.

briefs.influencer:
## Objective
Ride World Cup 2026 knockout-stage mania with a lighthearted, non-forced cleanup angle tied to watch parties — driving brand awareness and UGC for P&G Home Care during peak cultural attention, while staying tonally respectful given mixed sentiment around recent news (Jayden Adams' death). No jokes, no tie-ins to the tragedy — content strictly celebrates fan culture and game-day mess.

## Which P&G brand
Primary: **Dawn** (grease-cutting party snack spills, wing sauce, chip grease) and **Swiffer** (fast floor cleanup between goals/halftime).
Secondary: **Febreze** (stinky jersey/cleat/couch odor after a sweaty watch party) and **Mr. Clean** (post-party deep clean, muddy floors from backyard viewing setups).

## Target creators
- Sports/lifestyle creators who host watch parties (micro to mid, 50K–1M)
- Family & home creators who do game-day entertaining content
- College/young adult "game day fit + snack setup" creators
- Soccer-adjacent creators (fan culture, jersey/kit content) — avoid pundits/analysts commenting on player deaths or controversy
- Multi-platform: TikTok/IG Reels for chaos-to-clean transitions, YouTube Shorts/long for "watch party setup" vlogs, X for real-time reactive banter

## Key message
"World Cup watch parties go big — the mess goes bigger. [Brand] handles the chaos so you don't miss a second of the match."

## Hook / opening
- "Halftime score: Spills 3, Me 0." *(cut to Dawn/Swiffer save)*
- "POV: Your team scores and your guac bowl doesn't survive the celebration."
- "Rating watch party disasters until the final whistle 🏆🧽"

## Do
- Keep tone fun, chaotic, celebratory — mirror real fan energy
- Show authentic watch-party scenarios: spilled drinks, dropped snacks, muddy cleats by the door, jersey funk
- Use quick before/after or chaos-to-clean transitions
- Nod to all three host countries' fan cultures where relevant (US/Mexico/Canada)
- Stay reactive to match schedule (post around key knockout games/final)

## Don't
- Do NOT reference Jayden Adams' death, injuries, or any tragedy — zero crossover with that news thread
- Don't force product into commentary on match results, players, or controversies
- Don't overproduce — should feel like real fan content, not an ad
- Don't disparage any country, team, or fanbase
- Avoid political/rivalry flashpoints tied to host nations

## Suggested content
1. "Watch Party Fail Compilation" — funny mess moments → quick clean save
2. "Get Ready With Me: Watch Party Setup" — Swiffer pre-clean, Febreze on couch/pillows before guests arrive
3. "Halftime Cleanup Challenge" — race the clock during halftime break
4. "Jersey Rescue" — muddy backyard kickabout jersey → Dawn stain fight
5. Reactive X thread/posts during final: "Every World Cup watch party has THIS moment 👇"

## H
briefs.post:
## Platform & format
TikTok/IG Reels primary (15–20s vertical video), cross-post to X as a short clip + still to YouTube Shorts. Format: fast-cut "watch party chaos" montage → clean-up save.

## Caption (ready to post)
Final whistle. Salsa on the rug. Cleat mud on the tile. Jersey somehow has BBQ sauce on it and you don't even remember eating BBQ.
World Cup fever hits different when your living room becomes the stadium. 🏆🧽
We got you for extra time.

## Hook (first line / first 2s)
"GOAAAL— wait, who spilled the queso?!"

## Visual direction
Open on chaotic watch-party energy: friends jumping up celebrating, drink flying, chip bowl tipping onto the rug, muddy cleats tracked across the kitchen. Quick-cut to Dawn cutting grease off the jersey stain, Swiffer gliding over the muddy footprints, Febreze getting spritzed on the couch cushions before the next match starts. End on clean space, fresh jerseys hung up, everyone back on the couch for kickoff — product bottles casually in frame, no hard sell.

## Hashtags
#WorldCup2026 #WatchParty #GameDayMess #DawnDishSoap #SwifferSquad #FebrezeFresh #CleanUpCrew #FinalStretch

## CTA
Drop your worst watch-party disaster below 👇 — we'll tell you which P&G MVP saves it.

trends.title: Pink Stuff Paste Still Owns Cleaning TikTok
trends.summary: The UK-born pink paste continues to anchor 'satisfying restoration' cleaning content, with creators using it on ovens, pans and grout as a scrappy alternative to traditional multi-surface cleaners.
trends.angle: Mr. Clean can reclaim share of 'satisfying before/after' cleaning content by leaning into Magic Eraser and multipurpose spray in head-to-head restoration challenges.
trends.response: Seed Mr. Clean Magic Eraser and multipurpose spray with cleaning/restoration creators in side-by-side 'toughest grime' challenge videos to reassert credibility in the satisfying-cleaning niche.

examples.appeal: Before/after grime-removal reveals with a satisfying wipe-away moment drive shares and comments.
Head-to-head product comparison format taps into debate/curiosity, boosting engagement.
Aesthetic pastel packaging and tidy 'cleaning caddy' flatlays perform well as saveable Reels/carousels.

briefs.influencer:
## Objective
Reclaim "satisfying before/after" cleaning content share from Pink Stuff paste by positioning Mr. Clean Magic Eraser + Multipurpose Spray as the faster, less-scrubbing alternative for ovens, pans, and grout restoration.

## Which P&G brand
Mr. Clean (Magic Eraser + Multipurpose Spray combo)

## Target creators
- Mid-tier cleaning/restoration creators (50K–500K) known for satisfying transformation content
- Grout, oven, and cookware "before/after" niche accounts
- 2-3 "cleanfluencers" who've previously featured Pink Stuff, for head-to-head credibility
- Home creators with high rewatch/completion rates (algorithm favors this format)

## Key message
"Why scrub a paste in when you can erase it out?" — Magic Eraser + Multipurpose Spray deliver the same satisfying restoration with less elbow grease, no mixing, no mess.

## Hook / opening
Open mid-scrub: split screen, Pink Stuff paste on one side (still rubbing after 20 sec), Magic Eraser on the other (already lifting grime). Text overlay: "Same result. Half the effort. Let's settle this."

## Do
- Frame as head-to-head test, not disparagement — let visuals do the talking
- Use identical dirty surfaces (oven rack, pan, grout line) for fair split-test
- Show real-time or sped-up scrub effort side-by-side (time-stamped)
- Highlight zero pre-mixing/water needed for Magic Eraser
- End on tactile satisfaction shot — wipe reveal, close-up texture change
- Include Multipurpose Spray as the "finishing layer" for shine/disinfecting claim

## Don't
- Don't trash-talk Pink Stuff by name in disparaging tone — keep it a fair, fun challenge
- Don't over-produce; keep it raw/handheld to match trend's scrappy aesthetic
- Don't skip the tactile ASMR sound design (scrubbing, wiping) — it's the retention driver
- Don't make it an ad-read; let the transformation carry the message

## Suggested content
1. **"Pink vs. Clean" Challenge** — same dirty oven rack, split-screen restore-off
2. **Grout Line Duel** — timed side-by-side, caption reveals winner by seconds saved
3. **One-Wipe Pan Test** — burnt pan, single Magic Eraser pass vs. paste + scrub brush
4. **Duet/Stitch bait** — post the challenge unresolved, invite creators to stitch their own results

## Hashtags & CTA
#MrCleanChallenge #EraserVsPaste #SatisfyingClean #CleanTok
CTA: "Duet your dirtiest surface — Magic Eraser or paste? Let's see who wins."

## KPI
- 15M+ combined views across TikTok/IG in 30 days
- 500+ creator duets/stitches on challenge
- 20% lift in Magic Eraser branded search/mentions
- Sentiment ≥70% positive in comments (non-disparaging tone maintained)
briefs.post:
## Platform & format
TikTok primary (Reel cross-post to Instagram) — side-by-side split-screen restoration challenge, 15-25 sec, trending "satisfying cleaning" audio.

## Caption (ready to post)
Pink paste who? 🩷➡️🤍 Same crusty pan, same grimy oven rack, one Magic Eraser. Watch the grease tap out in seconds — no scrubbing marathon required. Some things don't need a trend to work. They just need to work.

## Hook (first line / first 2s)
"Everyone's obsessed with the pink stuff… so we grabbed the toughest pan we could find and let Mr. Clean answer back."

## Visual direction
Split screen: left side shows a heavily baked-on pan/oven rack, right side identical grime. Timer starts. One side uses Magic Eraser (quick circular motion, minimal effort), reveal at same timestamp — clean surface, still shiny, zero residue. End on close-up macro shot of eraser disintegrating grime like sandpaper on butter. Overlay text: "Same result. Half the elbow grease." Finish with Mr. Clean bottle + Magic Eraser both in frame, soft studio light, satisfying wipe-down slow-mo.

## Hashtags
#MrClean #MagicEraser #CleanTok #BeforeAndAfter #SatisfyingClean #CleaningHacks #PinkStuffChallenge #DeepClean

## CTA
Duet this with your dirtiest pan. Let's see if the eraser can out-clean the paste. 👇

trends.title: Scrub Daddy Sponge Remains a Cleaning-Haul Staple
trends.summary: The smiley FlexTexture sponge keeps recurring in cleaning-caddy hauls and ASMR scrubbing videos, with seasonal colorways and collabs refreshing interest without needing new launches.
trends.angle: Dawn can stay central to the ritual by pairing with whatever sponge is trending, reinforcing that the soap—not the sponge—is what actually cuts grease.
trends.response: Seed Dawn dish soap alongside trending sponges (including Scrub Daddy) in 'grease-cutting test' and satisfying dishwashing TikToks so Dawn rides the sponge trend instead of competing with it.

examples.appeal: Texture-change gimmick (soft in warm water, firm in cold) gives a built-in visual hook for short-form video.
Product haul format with bright colors and tidy organization is highly saveable/shoppable content.
Smiley-face branding photographs well, making it a recognizable prop in kitchen aesthetic Reels.

briefs.influencer:
## Objective
Ride the Scrub Daddy cleaning-haul wave to reinforce Dawn as the essential "cuts-through-grease" partner in any caddy — not competing with the sponge trend, but anchoring it.

## Which P&G brand
Dawn

## Target creators
- Cleaning-caddy/haul creators (10K–500K followers) on TikTok/IG Reels
- ASMR scrubbing/satisfying-clean niche accounts
- Home organization & "clean girl era" lifestyle creators
- Budget/dollar-store haul creators who feature Scrub Daddy colorways

## Key message
The sponge sets the vibe, but Dawn does the work. Any Scrub Daddy colorway + Dawn = the duo that actually cuts grease. Sponge trends come and go seasonally — Dawn's grease-cutting power is the constant.

## Hook / opening
"New Scrub Daddy color, same secret weapon 👀" or "Everyone's obsessed with the sponge... nobody talks about what's actually doing the work."

## Do
- Show Scrub Daddy (any colorway/collab) paired with Dawn in caddy/haul shots
- Use close-up "cut through grease" b-roll — dish, pan, or stovetop before/after
- Let ASMR scrub sounds play out naturally; Dawn suds visual is key beat
- Reference seasonal Scrub Daddy drops authentically (new color = fresh excuse to restock Dawn too)
- Keep tone playful, not preachy — sponge is the star, Dawn is the quiet MVP

## Don't
- Don't disparage Scrub Daddy or any specific brand
- Don't oversell with hard claims ("kills 99.9%…") — keep it grease-cutting, dish-specific
- Don't make it feel like an ad-read; integrate into existing haul/ASMR format
- Don't ignore the sponge trend — piggyback, don't compete

## Suggested content
1. "Caddy refresh" haul: new Scrub Daddy colorway unboxing + Dawn refill, ending on a satisfying grease-cut close-up
2. ASMR pan-scrub video: Scrub Daddy texture sounds + Dawn suds build-up, slow-mo rinse reveal
3. Myth-check format: "It's not the sponge, it's the soap" side-by-side grease test (Dawn vs. water-only)
4. Seasonal tie-in: "New [color] Scrub Daddy just dropped, but here's what's staying in my caddy no matter what"

## Hashtags & CTA
#DawnCutsGrease #CleaningCaddy #ScrubDaddy #CleanTok #SatisfyingClean
CTA: "Tag your caddy essentials — new sponge, same Dawn."

## KPI
- Video views & completion rate (target: top-quartile for cleaning-haul niche)
- Engagement rate (saves/shares indicate haul-list utility)
- Comment sentiment mentioning Dawn specifically (grease-cutting callouts)
- Branded hashtag usage / UGC pickup within 2 weeks
briefs.post:
## Platform & format
TikTok primary (15-25s cleaning ASMR/haul-style video), repurposed as Instagram Reel + static carousel for feed. Duet/stitch-friendly format so people can drop their own Scrub Daddy hauls into the trend.

## Caption (ready to post)
The sponge gets the glow-up. The soap does the actual work.

Scrub Daddy can change colors every season — Dawn's job stays the same: cutting grease on contact so that smiley face isn't scrubbing for its life.

Pair your favorite sponge with the one thing that makes it actually work. 🧡🫧

## Hook (first line / first 2s)
"Cute sponge. But have you checked what's IN the bottle?"

## Visual direction
Open on a satisfying overhead shot of a cleaning caddy haul — seasonal-colorway Scrub Daddy front and center, aesthetically arranged. Hand squirts Dawn onto the sponge, close-up macro shot of suds blooming instantly on a greasy pan. Quick ASMR scrub-and-rinse sequence, water sheeting off clean, grease-free surface. End on hero shot: Dawn bottle + Scrub Daddy side by side, text overlay: "The duo, not just the sponge."

## Hashtags
#Dawn #ScrubDaddy #CleaningHaul #CleanTok #GreaseCuttingPower #DawnDish #CleaningASMR #KitchenHacks

## CTA
Drop your Scrub Daddy colorway below 👇 then tell us — is it really clean without Dawn?

trends.title: Hypochlorous-Acid 'Clean With Electricity' Gains Ground
trends.summary: Hypochlorous acid systems marketed as bleach-free, pet- and baby-safe disinfectant alternatives keep gaining traction among clean-label and crunchy-living audiences skeptical of traditional chemical cleaners.
trends.angle: Clean-label skepticism toward bleach/quat disinfectants is an opening for Mr. Clean and Febreze to publish transparent, science-backed safety content rather than cede the 'natural' narrative.
trends.response: Publish creator- and expert-backed content on Mr. Clean/Febreze channels addressing 'is my disinfectant safe' questions head-on, pairing EPA-registration facts with real-use demos to counter DIY hypochlorous claims.

examples.appeal: The 'make your own disinfectant with electrolyzed water' process video is inherently novel and educational, driving watch-through.
Framed as a science hack, appealing to curiosity and DIY-savings audiences.
Clean, minimalist aesthetic content targets wellness-minded followers who share for identity signaling.

briefs.influencer:
## Objective
Intercept the "clean with electricity" / HOCl trend before Mr. Clean and Febreze get cast as the "chemical bad guy." Own the transparency lane: show our formulas' safety data, explain how HOCl actually works, and give clean-label-curious audiences a credible reason to trust EPA-registered disinfectants alongside (not instead of) natural alternatives.

## Which P&G brand
Mr. Clean (surface disinfecting authority) + Febreze (air/fabric safety angle). Lead with Mr. Clean; Febreze as secondary voice for pet/baby-safe odor claims.

## Target creators
- Micro/mid (20K–300K) "crunchy but not crazy" home creators who already use HOCl, vinegar, castile soap content
- Chemistry/science communicators (the "let me explain the pH" niche)
- Pet-parent and new-parent home creators focused on safety-first cleaning
- Avoid mega-influencers with existing "toxic-free brand" sponsorships — credibility risk

## Key message
"Natural" and "safe" aren't the same thing — and neither is "chemical" and "dangerous." Mr. Clean's formulas are tested, EPA-registered, and dermatologist/pediatrician reviewed. We're not afraid of the ingredient list — here it is, here's what it does, and here's the science.

## Hook / opening
"I made my own hypochlorous acid cleaner... then I asked an actual chemist if it's better than what's under my sink."
or
"Bleach, quats, HOCl — what's actually the safest for your kitchen counter? A chemist ranks them."

## Do
- Show real ingredient transparency (screenshot SDS/label, explain in plain English)
- Compare HOCl mechanism vs. Mr. Clean's disinfecting mechanism factually, no disparaging
- Feature EPA List N registration, contact-time accuracy, pet/baby-safe usage instructions
- Acknowledge the trend respectfully ("HOCl is genuinely interesting — here's the fuller picture")
- Use a real chemist, toxicologist, or formulator as a credibility anchor

## Don't
- Don't mock or dismiss HOCl/clean-living audiences — alienates the exact skeptics we need
- Don't make unverified superiority claims ("better than HOCl") — stick to facts + registered claims
- Don't use fear-based messaging about DIY HOCl machines
- Don't hide behind corporate jargon — no lab coats reciting marketing copy

## Suggested content
1. "Chemist Reacts" — creator breaks down HOCl generators vs. Mr. Clean ingredients, side by side
2. "What's Actually in My Cleaning Cabinet" — transparency haul, label read-through
3. Pet-owner test: Febreze fabric safety explained for homes with pets/newborns
4. "Ask a toxicologist" Q&A stitched from trending HOCl comments/skepticism
5. Myth vs Fact carousel (IG): "Bleach is bad" / "Quats cause resistance" / "Natural = safe" — fact-checked

## Hashtags & CTA
#CleanLabelFacts #ForceOfNature #MrCleanScience #SafeClean #AskTheChemist
CTA: "Check the label. Ask the science. Clean smarter." → Drive to P&G Home Care safety/ingredient transparency page.

## KPI
- Sentiment shift: track "trust/credibility
briefs.post:
## Platform & format
TikTok primary (talking-head + on-screen text overlay, lab demo cutaways), repurposed as Instagram Reel. 30-45 sec.

## Caption (ready to post)
"Electrolyzed water" isn't magic — it's chemistry. So is everything that actually disinfects your counters. We talked to our microbiologists about hypochlorous acid, why it's genuinely a cool disinfectant, and why "made with electricity" doesn't automatically mean safer or stronger than what's already in your cabinet. No shade to HOCl. Just facts, receipts, and a little myth-busting. 🧪✨ Clean should never require you to pick a side — just read the label. That's the whole Mr. Clean thing.

## Hook (first line / first 2s)
"Your new bleach-free spray was made... using electricity. Let's talk about what that actually means."

## Visual direction
- Open on creator holding up a trending HOCl spray bottle, genuinely curious (not mocking) tone
- Quick cut to whiteboard/kitchen counter "science corner" — Mr. Clean bottle next to HOCl bottle, no ranking, just side-by-side ingredient breakdown
- Overlay text pops: "HOCl = your white blood cells make this too" / "kills the same pathogens, different stability window"
- B-roll of surface swab test or UV-light germ demo for visual proof
- End on hand grabbing whichever bottle — VO: "Pick what works for your home. Just pick it with facts."

## Hashtags
#CleanTok #HypochlorousAcid #CleaningScience #MrClean #CleanLabel #DisinfectantFacts #HomeCareHacks

## CTA
Comment "SCIENCE" and we'll drop the full ingredient breakdown + link to our lab-safety FAQ. Follow for more clean-label myth-busting — we read the labels so you don't have to.

trends.title: Purple Fabuloso Scent Nostalgia Fuels Cleaning Content
trends.summary: Fabuloso's purple lavender scent keeps resurfacing as a nostalgia/meme touchpoint in cleaning TikToks, while clean-label challengers like Mrs. Meyer's, Method and Blueland ride a parallel 'cleaning as self-care' wave built on scent and pastel packaging.
trends.angle: Swiffer and Mr. Clean can borrow the scent-forward, aesthetic-packaging playbook driving Fabuloso and Mrs. Meyer's engagement to make routine mopping/surface cleaning feel more shareable.
trends.response: Seed a scent-led UGC push for Swiffer (limited-edition fragrance callouts) and 'what does your clean house smell like' TikToks that tie Swiffer/Mr. Clean scents into the same nostalgia and self-care framing.

examples.appeal: Nostalgic 'this smell = childhood/clean house' hook drives strong comment engagement and duets.
Pastel bottle flatlays and 'clean girl' room tours make the product a lifestyle prop, not just a cleaner.
Scent-and-vibe framing turns mundane cleaning into aspirational, saveable Reels content.

briefs.influencer:
## Objective
Ride the "purple Fabuloso nostalgia" wave to reposition Swiffer/Mr. Clean mopping as a sensory, aesthetic ritual—not a chore—driving trial of scent-forward SKUs and social shareability.

## Which P&G brand
Swiffer (WetJet / PowerMop scented solutions) — lead brand. Mr. Clean (Clean Freak scented mist) as secondary tie-in for surface cleaning content.

## Target creators
- Mid-tier cleaning/lifestyle TikTokers (50K–500K) known for ASMR/satisfying clean content
- "Cleantok" nostalgia creators who've done Fabuloso/scent-memory videos
- Home aesthetic + pastel/organization creators (crossover with Mrs. Meyer's audience)
- Multicultural household creators (Fabuloso's core nostalgia base skews Latino) — prioritize authentic overlap, not appropriation

## Key message
"The scent IS the clean." Mopping should smell as good as it looks — Swiffer turns routine floor care into a sensory reset, no bucket, no nostalgia trade-off required.

## Hook / opening
"POV: you finish mopping and the whole house smells like a memory." 
or
"Tell me you grew up on purple Fabuloso without telling me... now watch this scent upgrade."

## Do
- Lean into nostalgia/scent-memory storytelling before product reveal
- Show pastel/aesthetic packaging shots, satisfying before/after floor shine
- Use ASMR audio (mop swish, spray mist, cap pop)
- Let creators pick their own "core scent memory" angle (childhood home, grandma's house, etc.)
- Tag P&G brand handle + disclose partnership per FTC guidelines

## Don't
- Don't disparage Fabuloso/Mrs. Meyer's by name or knock competitor nostalgia
- Don't force scripted "chore = self-care" language — keep it organic
- Don't overproduce; low-fi, phone-shot aesthetic outperforms studio polish here
- Don't skip the disclosure tag

## Suggested content
1. "Scent memory" story time while mopping (voiceover nostalgia + satisfying visuals)
2. Aesthetic flatlay/GRWM-style "clean routine" featuring Swiffer + candle + playlist
3. Duet/stitch trending purple-Fabuloso nostalgia videos with a "my upgrade" reveal
4. Mr. Clean quick-mist surface refresh as a "5-min reset" satisfying clip

## Hashtags & CTA
#ScentFirstClean #SwifferGlowUp #CleanTok #MrCleanFreak 
CTA: "What scent takes you back? Duet this with your core cleaning memory."

## KPI
- Views, engagement rate, and shares on branded/duet content
- Volume of organic duets/stitches referencing brand hashtag
- Sentiment tracking (nostalgia-positive mentions)
- Click-through/trial lift on scented SKU product pages within 30 days
briefs.post:
## Platform & format
TikTok primary (15–20s POV/ASMR mop clip), cross-post to IG Reels + Stories

## Caption (ready to post)
that purple bottle raised us. now let's talk about the mop 💜

Mr. Clean's Lavender + Febreze Freshness isn't just clean, it's a whole mood. scent-first cleaning era = officially open

## Hook (first line / first 2s)
"POV: you're not cleaning, you're 'setting the vibe'" (text on screen, mop swirling into frame, purple sudsy water close-up)

## Visual direction
- Open tight on pouring purple Mr. Clean into mop bucket, slow-mo swirl, steam/mist effect
- Cut to floor reflection shot, soft pastel lighting, ASMR mop sounds + bottle clink
- Overlay text: "the smell hit before the nostalgia did" 
- End on clean floor + product bottle styled next to candle/plant for that "self-care shelfie" aesthetic
- Warm, pastel-toned color grade — think Mrs. Meyer's aesthetic but Mr. Clean strength

## Hashtags
#CleanTok #ScentFirst #MrClean #FebrezeFresh #CleaningASMR #PurpleCleanerEra #SelfCareSunday #MoppingMood

## CTA
Comment your cleaning scent era 👇 (then duet with your own mop routine — best one gets reposted)
